name: Apple Dictation
description: Built-in on-device dictation across macOS and iOS

developer.comment: Built into macOS and iOS by Apple.

first-release.comment: Dictation first shipped on the iPhone 4S / iPad (iOS, late 2011) and reached the Mac with OS X 10.8 Mountain Lion in July 2012. Modern on-device dictation arrived with macOS Ventura / iOS 16 (2022).

status.comment: Actively shipped and updated as a core macOS/iOS feature.

license.comment: Bundled, closed-source feature of Apple's operating systems.

primary-type.comment: Real-time speak-to-type input method; not a file/meeting transcriber.

real-time-dictation.comment: Live speak-to-type; words appear as you talk, with optional auto-punctuation.

file-transcription.comment: Dictation only handles live microphone input; it cannot import or transcribe existing audio/video files. (Apple's Voice Memos has separate transcription, but that is not Dictation.)

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting bot or system-audio capture; microphone-only dictation.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Produces a running transcript of your speech in near-real-time as you dictate.

audio-sources.comment: Microphone only; no file upload, system-audio, meeting-bot, or URL import.

underlying-engine.comment: Apple's own automatic speech recognition (the same models behind Siri/Dictation); on Apple silicon recent versions use Apple's on-device speech models. Not Whisper.

language-count.comment: Approx. Apple does not publish a single count; multiple sources cite roughly 30-50 languages/locales, with on-device processing available for a subset on Apple silicon. Checked 2026-06-12.

english-accuracy.comment: No published WER. Reviews generally rate Apple Dictation as solid and reliable on clear English speech but not best-in-class versus Whisper-based tools, especially on accents, long-form, or technical vocabulary. Rated 4 as a defensible mid-high estimate; no formal independent benchmark found.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: The dictation language must be selected; there is no automatic mixed-language auto-detection within a single dictation session.

custom-vocabulary.comment: No user-facing custom dictionary or vocabulary-boosting mechanism documented for Dictation.

timestamps.comment: Inserts plain text into the focused field; no timestamps.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Dictation only produces text; no summaries or notes.

built-in-editor.comment: Text is typed directly into whatever app has focus; there is no dedicated transcript editor.

translation.comment: Dictation transcribes speech; it does not translate the resulting text (Apple Translate is a separate app).

export-formats.comment: No transcript export; dictated text simply lands in the active text field of any app. No standalone document is produced to export.

processing-location.comment: On Apple silicon many languages are processed fully on-device; on Intel Macs or unsupported languages, audio is sent to Apple servers. Keyboard Settings indicates whether a given setup is on-device.

offline-capable.comment: On Apple silicon with a supported language and the language model downloaded, dictation runs on-device and works offline. Intel Macs and unsupported languages require a network connection.

data-used-for-training.comment: By default dictation is not stored or used for training; audio/transcripts are used to improve Siri & Dictation only if the user opts in via Settings > Privacy > Analytics & Improvements > Improve Siri & Dictation.

data-deletion-control.comment: Users can delete stored Siri & Dictation history via Settings > Siri > Siri & Dictation History > Delete.

platforms.comment: Built into macOS and iOS/iPadOS. Not available on Windows, Android, web, or Linux.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Once enabled in Keyboard settings, Dictation injects text into any focused text field across the OS.

integrations.comment: No meeting/app integrations; it works as a system input method, not via Zoom/Meet/Teams/Slack connectors.

api-automation.comment: Dictation itself exposes no user-facing API or CLI. (Apple's separate Speech framework is a developer API but is not the Dictation feature.)

pricing-model.comment: Included free with macOS and iOS; no subscription or purchase.

free-tier.comment: Entirely free as a built-in OS feature.

free-allowance.comment: No usage caps; free with the operating system.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free built-in feature covering full use; no paid tier.
